steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The finding shows clam gardens described in songs produced larger, more abundant clams despite increased harvesting. The correct choice must connect this evidence to what it demonstrates about Indigenous ecological knowledge.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'songs are a defining characteristic of clam cultivation': The passage shows songs preserve knowledge of cultivation—not that songs are a defining feature of the practice itself. Unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Indigenous communities harvested clams for cultural reasons': Focuses on motivation, not what the growth data actually demonstrates. ✗
  • Choice C — 'harvesting pressure changes clam species composition': The finding describes size and abundance increases, not species change. Contradicted by the data. ✗
  • Choice D — 'effective cultivation methods are among the ecological knowledge preserved in Indigenous songs': The clam gardens are cultivation methods for a food source, they were preserved in songs, and the growth data confirms they worked. This directly states what the finding demonstrates. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
